steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence's main verb is "became" ("Such meticulous documentation… became Kawara's life's work"). The blank comes after "information" and before the main verb, so it must not introduce a second competing main verb — it should modify the documentation without creating a new independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘information, has generated’: "has generated" is a finite main verb, creating two main verbs ("has generated" and "became") joined only by a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘information generates’: "generates" is also a finite main verb, same comma-splice problem without even the comma. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘information, generating’: the comma introduces a participial phrase ("generating artworks…") that modifies the subject without competing with "became" as a second main verb. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘information generated’: "generated" without a comma reads as a past-tense main verb, again giving the sentence two main verbs. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
